conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. 

Let me start with the basics: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex. Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who might also consider themselves responsible for him (for example, another parent or guardian)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Does Alex live with you full-time, or does he spend time living with someone else (for example, another parent)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just one final question to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Is Alex currently in full-time education (which would be normal for an 8-year-old), or are there any special circumstances such as him being in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's in full-time education - just at the local primary school. There are no special circumstances or anything like that. He's not in hospital or care or anything.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You are responsible for Alex and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- Alex is in full-time education at primary school
- There are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care arrangements)

Based on the official UK government eligibility criteria, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit:
- You are responsible for a child under 16
- You live in the UK
- The child lives with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for the same child

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (he's 8)
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and he lives with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive the payments or choose to opt out of receiving them while still getting National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives full-time with the claimant in the UK, is in full-time education, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives with him. All eligibility criteria are met: the claimant is responsible for a child under 16, they live in the UK, and only one person is claiming for the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for the same child. There are no special circumstances that would affect eligibility.

The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives with him. There are no special circumstances that would affect eligibility.
